latest.title: Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 14:30
RNS Number : 0835N
Atlas Metals Group PLC
20 July 2026
20 July 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company, announces that it will issue further ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") pursuant to the Company's
"at-the-market" facility with Axis Capital Markets Limited ("
Axis
"; the
"
At-the-Market Facility
"
)
.
At-the-Market Facility issue
2,640,000 new Ordinary Shares ("
ATM Shares
") will be issued at an average price of 7.04 pence per ATM Share, in connection with the At-the-Market Facility. As announced by the Company on 5 March 2026 all proceeds from the
At-the-Market Facility will go towards repayment of the funding facility currently in place with YA II PN Ltd, an institutional investor managed by Yorkville Advisors Global, LP.
Admission and total voting rights
Application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 2,640,000 ATM Shares to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission is expected to occur on or around 21 July 2026.
When issued, the ATM Shares will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 46,529,001
Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 46,529,001. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Update on Proposed Acquisition
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1292L
Atlas Metals Group PLC
06 July 2026
6 July 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Update on Proposed Acquisition
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company
,
is pleased to provide an update in relation to the Company's proposed acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd ("
UPSA
") (the "
Proposed Acquisition
").
The process to complete the Proposed Acquisition continues to progress and the directors of Atlas Metals and UPSA are now targeting completion by the end of Q3/beginning of Q4 2026, subject to,
inter
alia, the approval of the prospectus by the FCA and approval by Atlas Metals shareholders.
SLR Consulting Australia Pty Ltd ("
SLR
"), which is engaged by the Company, is continuing to work on an updated Competent Person's Report ("
CPR
") using data and results of testing of samples obtained from the drilling campaign undertaken earlier this year, with the target of upgrading the resources from the inferred to measured and indicated. The Company expects to publish an updated CPR in the coming weeks and ahead of completion of the Proposed Acquisition.
Atlas Metals is pleased to announce that it intends to appoint Robert Ober as a strategic advisor to the Board on completion of the Proposed Acquisition. Robert Ober is a highly respected figure in the United States concrete and construction materials industry and currently serves as Chairman of the UPSA Advisory Board. Over a career spanning several decades, Mr. Ober has founded and developed multiple high-performance concrete and construction materials businesses, building extensive expertise in concrete production, aggregates supply chains, batching technologies, and large-scale infrastructure supply. Robert is widely recognised for his deep industry relationships across the United States construction sector and is regarded as a leading authority on the operational and commercial dynamics of high-performance concrete markets.
In addition to the Proposed Acquisition, Atlas Metals and UPSA continue to engage with potential customers and off-takers of the UPSA resource as well as advancing regulatory approvals in Australia.
For further information, please contact:

Atlas Metals Group plc

Company
")
Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company
,
in conformity with the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1R, announces that, as at 1 July 2026, its issued share capital comprised 43,889,001 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
"). Each Ordinary Share carries the right to one vote in relation to all circumstances at general meetings of the Company. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury and therefore the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 43,889,001.
The above figure of 43,889,001 may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: AMG Ltd is a small, exploration‑focused company that owns and develops uranium assets in Kyrgyzstan. Its core activity is locating, drilling and preparing deposits for future production.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is the fuel for nuclear power, a sector that is expected to grow as nations look for low‑carbon energy. AMG’s assets, while modest, give it a foothold in a market that could provide long‑term value if the company can secure the capital and approvals needed to move forward.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still pre‑revenue and heavily cash‑burning. It has recently issued new ordinary shares and is in the midst of a reverse takeover that could give it a public listing. The share price is at the very bottom of its valuation, effectively a penny stock.

rns_narrative: AMG is working through a reverse takeover, which requires a full FCA‑approved prospectus. The company has issued new shares and is seeking a funding facility to support its exploration programme.
Governance updates – AGM results, auditor changes, and director dealings – suggest the board is actively managing the company’s structure and finances.
Operationally, the focus is on securing the Kyrgyz uranium asset and completing the share issue to raise the capital needed for drilling and development.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Cautious hope – investors are waiting for the reverse takeover to clear regulators and for a share issue to provide liquidity.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders see a chance for a big jump if the RTO is approved and the new shares float. They point to the low price and the potential upside of uranium demand.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others are worried about possible fraud claims, slow regulatory approval, and the lack of a clear path to revenue. They fear the company may never gain enough traction to justify the current valuation.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from skepticism to a tentative optimism as the company announces new share issues and a funding facility, but the overall mood remains cautious.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for AMG is flat – no recent search activity, which is a sign that the market is largely unaware and a surprise catalyst could trigger a move.

near_term_catalysts: Approval of the FCA prospectus for the reverse takeover, which would allow the company to list and access a wider investor base.
Completion of the new share issue and a clear statement of the total voting rights and capital structure.
Any announcement that the Kyrgyz uranium asset has moved from exploration to a development stage, indicating progress toward revenue.
A significant spike in trading volume that breaks the current support level, signalling that smart money is starting to accumulate.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume surge of at least 200% above the 20‑day average, a price break above the 0.05p support level, and any regulatory update on the prospectus or funding facility.
expect_next.timing_regime: The company appears to be in the early phase of a recovery cycle – the APEX score suggests moderate compression but the lack of a clear catalyst means the mean reversion could take several months. A breakout on volume would be the most convincing confirmation.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the reverse takeover clears regulatory hurdles and the new shares float, the share price could rebound to at least the midpoint of its 52‑week range. Given the current price is essentially zero, a recovery to 0.05p would represent a 5,000% return. The deep discount and potential for uranium demand make the upside attractive if the company can move past the current uncertainty.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company faces real risks: delayed or denied prospectus approval, potential fraud allegations, and continued low liquidity. If the reverse takeover fails or the funding facility is not secured, the share could remain at the current level or fall further, leaving investors with a near‑total loss.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is extremely high relative to the minimal downside (the share could fall to zero). The risk/reward ratio is therefore highly asymmetric, but the lack of a concrete catalyst keeps the position speculative.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above 0.05p with a volume spike of at least 200% over the 20‑day average. Use a small position size – no more than 1% of the portfolio – and set a stop 0.02p below entry to protect against a short‑term reversal.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the FCA prospectus is approved or the new share issue is completed. If either event occurs and the price rises above 0.05p, consider a partial or full entry.
playbook.if_passing: If no progress on the prospectus or share issue is made by the end of the third quarter, consider exiting the position to preserve capital.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AMG is a deeply discounted uranium explorer awaiting a reverse takeover approval; if that catalyst clears, a mean‑reversion could deliver outsized returns, but the lack of a clear path and potential fraud risks make it a speculative play.
